findings.finding_text: Current --with-website release path checks website asset bytes but does not compare the latest registered website snapshot JSON against canonical LOVS output. New gate must cover this surface explicitly.
Help text for --with-website overstates the action: says 'Also run the website sync' even though the code path checks hazards and asset sync only. Should say 'check website parity'.
Daily prep website gates run focused website tests but do not independently prove the written latest snapshot still matches canonical LOVS after the website sync step. New gate should run alongside.

Website publisher exposes --lovs-root which is useful for review but allows accidental stale-root generation. This is exactly how the May-26 stale-worktree contamination occurred (lovs-tabled-2026-05-22 produced 106 while canonical held 112).
Existing lovs.cross_surface_parity module provides reusable asset byte-parity logic. Avoid duplication by composing instead of re-implementing.

Route date and analytic asOf must remain distinct in the gate. The gate must not require snapshot.date == canonical as_of[:10] (would block legitimate publication-state route advance when analytic data has not changed).
